title: ATC After Dark - THE FIFTH ELEMENT

venue.name: Zelifcam
venue.address: 10200 San Pedro Ave, San Antonio, TX, 78216

description:
**Big badaboom. We’re taking the Hackerspace outside.**
ATC is clearing the parking lot for an outdoor screening of the most gloriously chaotic sci-fi movie ever committed to film: The Fifth Element.
Bruce Willis, a flying-taxi future, Gary Oldman chewing every piece of scenery in sight, and Chris Tucker as the loudest radio host in the galaxy. It’s a vibe. Come watch it on a big screen under the stars with your fellow San Antonio devs and sci-fi enthusiasts.

## The Vibe

1. Outdoor screening in the ATC lot, lights, big screen, summer night
2. Bring a camp chair or a blanket. Bring a +1.
3. Popcorn machine on site. Drinks provided.

## What to Bring

1. A folding chair or blanket (we’ve got limited seating, so BYO is the move)
2. A light layer, June nights in SA can still surprise you
3. Bug spray if you’re the mosquito-magnet type

## Good To Know

1. Rated PG-13. Family/+1 friendly, but use your judgment for the little ones.
2. Starts 7:30 PM; film rolls at dusk (8:10 PM) once it’s dark enough to see the screen.
3. Weather call: if the forecast turns on us, we’ll move indoors; watch the Meetup + Discord for updates.

*This is what a tech community looks like when it logs off for a night. Pull up a chair.*
*Alamo Tech Collective*
RSVP so we know how much popcorn to make. 🍿

title: Byte Night

venue.name: Zelifcam
venue.address: 10200 San Pedro Ave, San Antonio, TX, 78216

description:
Byte Night is a laid-back gathering for programmers, tech folks, indie hackers, and curious minds in San Antonio who just want to hang out, talk shop, share ideas, and code together.

No pressure, no pitches — just good conversations about programming, business, tech trends, side projects, and whatever else comes up. Bring your laptop if you want to hack on something, or just come for the company and the chatter.

Whether you're building the next big thing, tinkering with personal projects, or just looking to meet other people who *get* it, you’re welcome here.

✨ **What to expect:**

* Chill vibes and great conversations
* People working on cool stuff (or nothing at all)
* Tech talk, idea-sharing, and occasional brainstorming
* Everyone from seasoned devs to beginners welcome
* Probably tacos or coffee involved (this is San Antonio, after all)

**Come hang out. Let’s talk code, life, and everything in between.**
**We have a donation area for the San Antonio Food Bank! Don't forget to bring non-perishable items to help out.**
